page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARM64 architecture with the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 design running at 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

In benchmark testing, the m8gn.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, highlighted by a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong uncached memory read and write bandwidths of 296.75 GB/sec and 138.43 GB/sec respectively. Single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores are strong, supported by efficient bzip3 decompression and gzip compression capabilities. Database operations also perform strongly, while LLM inference tasks show average to weak performance, particularly during prompt processing for medium-sized models.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, this instance is optimized for network-heavy workloads. The ARM-based architecture provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency compared to traditional x86 general-purpose instances. The absence of local storage means workloads must rely on network-attached EBS volumes. This server is highly suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, web serving, and network-intensive data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gn.4xlarge is an ARM64-based general-purpose server powered by 16 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance relies on network-attached storage but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate strong memory bandwidth, with uncached reads reaching 296.75 GB/sec, alongside strong database and decompression performance. The dedicated core allocation without hyperthreading ensures predictable execution. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for network-intensive and general-purpose workloads, making it highly suitable for database operations, web serving, and distributed applications requiring high network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, optimized for database and network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A network-optimized ARM64 server delivering dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for balanced general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 physical cores (AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2) at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
High-speed baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps
Strong memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
Optimized for network-heavy applications, web serving, and databases
No local storage or GPU, utilizing network-attached storage
